description.en: A bright orchard pup that sniffs out sweet fruit.
A smoky little cat with ember-bright eyes and a loyal streak.
A calm lake swimmer that flashes blue under clear water.
A dim marsh spirit that drifts over still water after sunset.
A thick-horned beast that can shove aside loose stone.
A small thorny trickster that darts through brush and laughs at fences.
A low, sturdy digger that vanishes under soft soil.
A long-bodied stone creature with a spine like linked iron.
A flame spirit that rolls over hot stone without burning out.
A dark-beaked bird that pecks at warm ash.
A sleepy owl that wakes only when the moon is high.
A warm-scaled snake that leaves tiny sparks in dry grass.
A lean fighter that circles until it finds an opening.
A clever red hunter that listens before it leaps.
An icy coil-beast that loops around rocks before striking.
A sharp frozen hunter that cuts clean tracks through drifts.
The rivergate dragon, ancient, brilliant, and far too proud to stay hidden.
A sure-footed grazer with small curved horns.
A heavy shadow wrapped in old armor and stubborn anger.
A broad-snouted swamp hunter that snaps from below the reeds.
A mud-splattered brute that charges from boggy ground.
A damp little water creature with a muddy grin.
A playful cat that turns every chase into a game.
A pale fox that blurs into morning fog.
A huge ridge guardian with paws like falling boulders.
A messy little crawler that loves wet leaves and trouble.
A dusk bird that disappears when lanterns flare.
A dark frost creature with a crest like frozen midnight.
A compact stone-backed creature that curls into a shield.
A crystal-shelled creature that glints in snowlight.
A black-winged omen that appears near quiet places.
A small frog that nests inside broken reeds and old pots.
A nimble river fish that flicks water like thrown glass.
A sharp-mouthed fish that guards narrow channels.
A bright winter bird that rides cold gusts.
A white-eared runner that bounds across crusted snow.
A chatty forest bird that answers whistles.
A mountain bruiser that uses its claws like climbing picks.
A steady rock-backed creature that wins by refusing to move.
A scrappy forest fighter that jabs before it thinks.
A twig-legged forest creature that freezes when watched.
A quick green serpent that hides between roots and hanging vines.
A scarred cave fighter that lowers its head and rushes.
Fire burns.
This is very hot.
A snowstorm is coming.
It's cold.
It was chilly.
Ice is very cold.
The wind blows.
Grass grew.
The tree falls.
It hurts.
That's water.
That animal is strong.
Attack them.
Water is good.
Tom fell into the water.
The fish swam in the lake.
Flies fly.
A standard pod for catching wild companions.
A reinforced pod with better odds against stubborn companions.
Restores a companion with sweet, field-packed fruit.
Clean spring water that restores a tired companion.
A local token used for small route shops and favors.

text.en: Take fruit before the road.
A fed companion fights longer.
You are safe. Rest here, then try again.
The rivergate burns with green fire. I am done hiding.
The storm is finally quiet.
Hot food helps after a long river walk.
A Snowbird circled my garden at dawn — first one in a decade.
The elders say a bonded Snowbird tells the sky when the frost is coming. Frostvale needs warning this year.
Catch one Snowbird and Frostvale keeps its garden through winter.
Birds circle when someone strays from the high path.
Watch the shadows, not just the road.
Rivers, you're standing at the Rivergate Approach. The dragon isn't far.
A companion you've truly bonded with can do more than fight — it can read what you're about to do before you do it.
Bond with one more water creature here. You'll want the proof before the dragon.
Frost glows blue before a storm.
Follow the lighted fences if you get turned around.
A hiker passed through yesterday — didn't come back down.
She was headed to Loren at the lake in Lakehaven. Said she'd bring a torch-path token as payment.
If you find her pack, carry what's inside to Loren at the lake. She needs the delivery done.
Breathe with the cave draft. It points toward safe stone.
Lakehaven listens better than it speaks. Sit by the water long enough and you'll hear it too.
The lake remembers every creature that comes to drink. Bond with two of them and it'll remember you too.
Catch two water creatures and come sit with me again.
Capture pods are packed.
Bring home anything you can care for.
Rivers! Glad the village is still here when you came home.
Have you heard the old story of the reedfrog? A bonded Reedfrog is what makes a house feel safe.
Bring one back and I'll make the spare room yours for good.
If the path narrows, check your supplies before you commit.
The final river bends toward old trouble.
If you hear wings, save your best throw.
The shop is open by the fountain.
Lake trainers compare catches at sunset.
Something wild jumps from the grass.
Captured. It settles into the pod.
It breaks loose and bolts.
The well runs cold even in summer.
Good water keeps companions steady.
Don't tell my parents I'm in here.
I want to catch a Stoneclaw. Or a mudgrub. Either one, whichever.
Dreadpeak doesn't forgive sloppy teams.
You carry three proofs. I felt them walk in with you.
Show me why the mountain should let you pass.
You climbed higher than I expected.
Four proofs. Four keys. I'm the last of the four wardens the chain was forged for.
The Rivergate is a stone door. Your proofs are the stones it wants. Go.
Rivers. Whatever you choose when you meet it — fight, catch, or walk up unarmed — I'll call it right.
The cavern's quiet again, Rivers. First time in forty years.
Something's been moving in the shadow at the back of the cavern.
Two cave-dwellers will tell us what it is — they see in the dark the way Rivers sees maps.
Catch two cave creatures and bring them back. We'll read the shadow together.
The shadow is on the move still. But it's not what's wrong.
The polished stones are. Count them. Four. Matches the shrine on Highridge exactly.
Cliff taught me to tend the braziers. Don't let them go out.
Mind the polished stones. Four of them. They matter more than they look.
Take the four. Open the gate. End this.
Cold hands fumble capture pods.
Warm them before you throw. And show me if anyone on the lake sent you — I'll read the braid.
Loren's braid. I lost my brother to this mountain the winter it was made.
Free spring tonic whenever you pass. Don't argue with me, Rivers.
Big catches live near deep water. Keep your team ready.
If you catch a Snowbird, bring it by the garden. I'm updating the cold-season roster.
Snowbird confirmed. Thank you, Rivers.
I wrote you a note for the Dreadpeak warden. It skips you past the first encounter wall.
I'm through Frostvale on my way home. Dreadpeak's colder than this.
Bring a light. A real one, with a named flame.
Told you it was colder. Give me your lantern — I'll relight it free.
Walk the torch path first. Then come find me if you want to map the shadows.
These torches mark the old climb.
When one goes dark, something big passed nearby. Keep yours named and burning.
Frostvale tests patience first.
Have some soup. Read Marin's letter while it warms.
That's a dragon path she's describing. They don't walk these in peace.
Show me your warmest heart.
You kept moving through the cold.
Carry this badge onward. And this lantern — Last Light. Dreadpeak eats flames that aren't named.
Cliff was the last of the four. Now there's only the Rivergate left.
Walk it with Last Light high. Don't put it out until you're home.
The soup's still warm, Rivers. Sit. Tell me what the dragon said.
Sit with me a minute. The bench is warmer than it looks.
I don't tell the four-warden story to people on their way up the mountain. Come back after.
Four wardens were chosen when the chain was forged. Tarrin at the stone. Marin at the water. Me for a season, then Frost.
Cliff at the cavern is the fourth. And there was a fifth, before my time. We don't say that name out loud.
Go to Highridge when you've time. The shrine stones remember four names. Look for the one that wasn't there yesterday.
Dad said the old dragon story is real this year.
He said it without a joke after. That's how I knew.
Greenwood Road runs thick with wild creatures this season.
Every bonded forest creature tells us something about what's moving through the trees.
Catch two from the forest biome and I'll read their signs with you.
The low trail looks harmless until the grass starts moving.
You dealt with Rook. Good. They needed the reminder.
I found odd tracks in the third grass patch. Too big for anything I know.
I watch the Greenwood crossings.
Something has been driving the wild ones toward town.
Good timing. I need field notes from Greenwood.
Catch two route creatures and I'll trade you a stronger pod.
You've made it past the village. Let's see what your companion can do.
I'm not going easy on you.
You handled yourself.
Greenwood Road is yours.
Tarrin's stones. I felt them hum when you came back past.
Something upstream is waking. Keep walking, Rivers.
You walked up to a dragon, Rivers. That's the sentence I'll tell.
Deep grass means wild creatures nearby.
If the grass rustles twice, back up or be ready.
I'm heading south. Tarrin teaches. They don't hurt.
Back up the ridge. I hear you're building a good team.
Tarrin will read your companions before they read you. Be honest with them.
The old shrine at the summit is missing its stones.
Only Pebbleback keeps shrine stones in its shell. Other stone-types leave them scattered.
Catch two Pebblebacks and the shrine will know your name.
Third switchback. The wind gets loud here.
Keep your hood tight or lose it.
My leg's done for today. I can't make the walk down to Lakehaven.
Take this marker to Loren on the pier. They'll send help up.
Loren's people carried me half the way home last night. You bought me a life, Rivers.
I run messages between the huts. I'm Tarrin's apprentice.
The middle stone is cracked. We don't know what cracked it. Tarrin doesn't either.
Tarrin said your team fought clean. That's a high compliment from them.
I carve names into the stones. For a small fee, mind.
Earn one proof and I'll carve yours for nothing.
Hold still, Rivers. The chisel knows your name already.
Four wardens, four stones. Someone told me there were five once.
The pass belongs to teams that can take a hit.
Stand firm.
You took the hit and kept going.
Highridge Pass is open.
Marin wrote me about the water. Her hand doesn't shake unless it's real.
The cracked stone has been cracked since before I was a warden. I still don't know what did it.
The stones have a fifth name on them now, Rivers. Read it when the sun is high.
The lake wind makes everyone slow down.
Catch two reed-edge creatures for my field count — what did you say? — the frogs, the frogs in the reeds.
My brother catches the big ones. I catch the fast ones.
The fish are moving inland. All of them. It's weird.
She tells everyone she catches the fast ones.
She's right. I weigh them, she names them. It works.
One trail token buys a cot and hot water. Cheap for a tired team.
The north door opens in winter. Don't try it in summer or the hinges will scream at you.
Lakehaven needs a runner with steady hands.
We carried your Highridge hiker down to the warm stones. Marin said you'd come through.
Marin is my sibling, by the way. She doesn't tell many people.
Marin never fights anyone she thinks will lose. She fought you anyway.
Here — a wind-braid. Brindle up in Frostvale will know what it means.
Lakehaven watches the water and the trainer.
Let's see if you can read both.
You stayed calm under pressure.
Take the lake badge. And take this letter to Frost; they should read it before you do.
Frost read the letter. I could feel it from here — the shift in the wind.
The fish are still moving inland. Whatever's upstream hasn't stopped.
The water's flat again, Rivers. You did that.
Piper sends letters about a kid named Rivers. That'd be you.
Her orchard-fruit recipe is older than our grandmother. I make it here with lake salt.
Fruit, pods, and tonics. Nothing fancy, all useful.
You're a good customer, Rivers. I'll start a tab if you want.
Rivers, today you start your own investigation.
Three creatures answered the call.
Choose the partner you trust at your side.
Good. Your first partner chose you back.
The eastern gate is open now.
The shrine stone cracked before I was a warden, Rivers.
Go to Lakehaven. The fishers there read the water better than I do.
You came home. That matters more than the dragon, Rivers.

beats.narrative: Player wakes in the starter village: a small grass-ringed home plot with no encounters. jan Sewi runs the starter ceremony; jan Pona Tomo, jan Telo Well, jan Poki Tomo, and jan Kili Tomo provide ambient two-beat village dialog. Walking up to jan Sewi fires the starter ceremony and offers the three starters: ashcat, mireling, and bramble_imp. Picking one sets starter_chosen, drops three capture_pod, adds the chosen creature to the party at level 5, and unlocks the eastern warp.

Long forest path-route east-to-west, framed by trees. Three tall-grass rectangles host the player's first encounters across the level 3-7 band: bramble_imp, thornling, mirthcat, applepup, mudgrub, nightjar, burrowmole, mistfox, bog_wisp, songbird, vine_adder, and hillbuck. Four ambient route walkers plus jan Ike bring the map to five NPC markers. Defeating jan Ike, or losing and restoring at the last village, opens the eastern warp.

Mountain pass with stone footpaths between blocked cliffs, rocks, and tall-grass clumps. Encounters skew force-typed and rocky: tarrin, chainback, mire_brute, quartz_shell, twiglet, drowsy_owl, ember_adder, and snowhare. Route NPCs provide ambient travel dialog. At the top, the Highridge master blocks the northward path. Winning sets badge_sewi and records the highridge-proof clue.

Round village built on a stone island in the middle of a lake. No tall grass and no random encounters: this is a story-and-shop beat. The Lakehaven master waits in the plaza; beating him sets badge_telo and records the lakehaven-proof clue. Lake-village NPCs fill the floor, and the stall sells capture pods and healing fruit for coins.

Cold-land village with snow and ice ground, stone paths, snow tall grass, trees, houses, rocks, and a fenced herb garden. Encounters mix frostcoil, quartz_shell, nightspike, snowbird, hillbuck, pebbleback, and glacier_talon at levels 7-12. The Frostvale master anchors the village challenge. Beating him sets badge_lete and records the frostvale-proof clue.

The great peak is the hardest region-master challenge before the endgame: a tall narrow cave-shrine with stone floors, blocked rock walls, torch landmarks, and sparse encounter overgrowth. Encounters are heavy elites: mountain_bear, warback, stoneclaw, fangrunner, glacier_talon, coalbeak, raven_shade, snowhare, foxhound, and boulderhorn at levels 10-15. Cave NPCs flesh out the climb before the Dreadpeak master. Beating him sets badge_suli and records the dreadpeak-proof clue.

Endgame approach: long riverside route with a grassy west bank, deep water channel, sandbar path, and two aquatic encounter patches. Encounters include bog_wisp, ashcat, mireling, bluefin, snapper, reedfrog, iron_wraith, riverfin, cinderling, marshjaw, and the rare green_dragon across levels 7-13. jan Kala Lili, jan Moku Pona, jan Kala Suli, jan Olin Telo, and jan Suno Telo populate the bank. With all four region badges in hand, final_boss_trigger fires the green_dragon cutscene; the green-dragon clear path plays the green dragon defeat animation.

regions.summary: Dark cavern with torchlight pools. Cliff — the last of the four valley wardens — waits in the final chamber. The confront-beat of the green-dragon mystery happens here.
flavor_beats: Wet cold, not dry like Frostvale. Drip-echoes constant.
The floor is stone, but every few steps there's a deeper stone — polished, set in a pattern.
The polished stones mirror the four-stone layout at Highridge. A careful walker notices.
At the final chamber, Cliff sits on a stone bench facing the entrance, waiting.

regions.summary: Snow-covered village with timber houses on stilts. Frost's trial-shrine is heated from inside. The word 'dragon' is spoken aloud for the first time in the game here.
flavor_beats: Smoke rises from every chimney.
A fenced herb garden on the north wall still grows in the snow.
When Rivers walks into Frost's shrine, steam blooms off their clothes.
Frost keeps a pot of soup on a stone brazier and offers a bowl before the fight.

regions.summary: Old trade route east of Riverside Home, flanked by Fan-tasy forest. Three tall-grass clumps host the player's first encounters. Rook is waiting at the east edge.
flavor_beats: A wooden footbridge crosses a dry creek halfway along the route.
Light filters through leaves the color of the valley's old flag.
At the east end a stone marker reads HIGHRIDGE PASS, NORTH-EAST.

regions.summary: Stone switchbacks climbing a grass-over-granite ridge. Tarrin's shrine sits at the top: four stones in a square, a fifth cracked in the middle. First region-master trial.
flavor_beats: The wind gets audible on the third switchback.
Crows overhead the whole climb.
Sun washes the shrine stones gold at the top.
The fifth stone — the middle one — has a claw-scar that doesn't match any catalog creature.

regions.summary: A small stone island in the center of a round lake, reached by a pier. Story-and-shop beat with no wild encounters inside the village. Marin the harbor-master anchors the region.
flavor_beats: The fog never fully burns off; lamps stay lit at noon.
The pier smells like fresh fish and spring-tonic herbs.
Reed beds on the shore host Reedfrogs.
Every stone in the island was quarried from Highridge two wardens ago.

regions.summary: Rivers's home village. Green-ringed, river-adjacent. The starter ceremony happens here.
flavor_beats: Clay-tile roofs. The same ones Selby was born under.
The well in the center is older than the village's oldest story.

body.en: Mind the torches. Mind the stones.

signs.title: NORTH PATH — FINAL CHAMBER / SOUTH PATH — LOOPS BACK

body.en: The loop is for the tired. The chamber is for the ready.
One of four. The shrine is twelve paces off, somewhere under the sky.
Warmth kept here since the cold came down.
Some plants only grow in winter.
Warm yourself before the bell rings.
Slow walkers welcome. Fast ones too.
Use the north ford when the ice comes.
The wind hits harder above the second switchback.
One of four, laid by the first wardens.
Built on the oldest stone in the valley.
Winter only. Walk the ice when it holds.
Where the river remembers your name.
Selby has lived here since before anyone else.
East out of the village. First rook east of the elm.
